prompt:
You are the Architect agent drafting a typed Method artifact for an architecture project, following Juval Lowy's The Method to the letter.
Respond with a single JSON object. No prose outside the JSON.
Target artifact: Glossary
Your JSON output MUST conform exactly to this JSON Schema. Emit every required field; for an optional object field, emit the object or null — never a bare string in its place.
Schema: {"properties":{"items":{"items":{"properties":{"term":{"type":"string"},"definition":{"type":"string"},"category":{"type":"string"}},"additionalProperties":false,"type":"object","required":["term","definition","category"]},"type":"array"}},"additionalProperties":false,"type":"object","required":["items"]}

Mission context:
{"vision":"A community where local events organize people effortlessly.","objectives":[{"number":1,"statement":"Sustain organizer retention above 70% quarter-over-quarter by making event coordination reliably effortless."},{"number":2,"statement":"Achieve a member RSVP-to-attendance conversion rate above 60% through timely, channel-agnostic engagement."},{"number":3,"statement":"Onboard new event categories and external data sources within two weeks without re-architecting the system."}],"mission":"Provide an events coordination system whose stable engagement core—orchestrating the evolving relationships between organizers, members, and events—remains insulated from volatile resources via pluggable channel, identity, and venue-data adapters, so the relationships the system cultivates endure as their underlying technologies change."}

Task: Extract the system's ubiquitous-language terms, each categorised by the Four Questions: Who interacts with the system, What is required of it, How (the business activity), Where (state lives). Define each term crisply in business language with NO solution/implementation wording. These terms are the shared vocabulary every later artifact must reuse verbatim.

items.definition: A person who creates and coordinates events for a community, responsible for cultivating participation and follow-through.
A person who discovers, commits to, and attends events within the community.
A scheduled community gathering around which organizers and members form relationships and coordinate participation.
An external party through which the community is reached and engaged across varied means of communication.
An external party that vouches for who a person is when they participate in the community.
An external party supplying information about places where events may be held.
The requirement to make organizing an event reliably effortless so organizers continue coordinating events over time.
The requirement to reach members in a timely, channel-agnostic way so commitments translate into attendance.
The requirement to admit new event categories and external information sources quickly without disrupting the established way relationships are cultivated.
The requirement that the relationships the system cultivates between organizers, members, and events persist independently of changing underlying technologies.
The business activity of cultivating and managing the evolving relationships between organizers, members, and events.
The business activity of reaching members and prompting them to commit to and show up for events.
The business activity of introducing a new event category, channel, identity source, or venue-data source into the community.
The business activity of moving a member from a stated intention to attend toward actual attendance.
A member's expressed commitment to attend a particular event.
The recorded fact that a member was present at an event.
A kind of event that the community recognizes and organizes around.
The held knowledge of the relationships and interactions among organizers, members, and events.
The held measure of organizers continuing to coordinate events from one period to the next.
The held measure of how often a member's RSVP results in actual attendance.
